market_summary.narrative: The week of March 31 – April 2, 2026 (a four-day week shortened by Good Friday) delivered broad equity gains despite an escalating U.S.-Iran military conflict and a sharp surge in oil prices. All three major indices advanced: the S&P 500 rose +3.36%, the Nasdaq gained +4.44%, and the Russell 2000 added +3.28%, though all three remain in negative YTD territory except the Russell 2000 (+1.94%). The dominant macro event was President Trump's April 1 evening address, in which he signaled continued and intensified military operations against Iran 'over the next two to three weeks,' sending WTI crude surging above $110/bbl — a level not seen since 2022 — as the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed to commercial tanker traffic. Paradoxically, equities rallied as investors interpreted the speech as clarifying the timeline rather than widening the conflict, reducing tail-risk uncertainty. The week's most notable earnings events were Nike (NKE), which fell -14% after reporting flat Q3 FY2026 revenue and guiding Greater China revenue down ~20% for Q4, and McCormick (MKC), which beat Q1 2026 estimates but declined -8% after simultaneously announcing a transformative merger with Unilever Foods. Technology led the recovery, with semiconductors and cybersecurity benefiting from AI infrastructure demand and heightened geopolitical security spending.

value_picks.name: Abbott Laboratories
value_picks.analyst_consensus: Strong Buy (11 analysts, 11 Buy/0 Neutral/0 Sell)
value_picks.why_undervalued: ABT suffered its worst single-day stock drop in over 20 years in January 2026 after Q4 2025 revenue of $11.46B missed estimates of $11.8B, primarily driven by an 8.9% decline in nutrition sales from WIC contract losses and pricing pressure on adult shakes. Ongoing litigation risk related to the NEC baby formula lawsuit and sector-wide rotation out of healthcare have compounded the selloff, pushing the stock down ~26% from its 52-week high to near a 52-week low.
value_picks.bull_case: Abbott's medical devices segment — including the FreeStyle Libre CGM franchise growing double digits — provides durable structural growth, and the nutrition headwind is transitory as WIC contract dynamics normalize by mid-2026. Management guided FY2026 adjusted EPS of $5.68 (in-line with consensus), and with $7.4B in TTM FCF at a 3.4% yield, the stock offers a high-quality defensive compounder at ~19.6x EV/EBITDA — a meaningful discount to its 5-year average.

value_picks.name: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: KDP is trading near its 52-week low as the market digests the complexity and leverage risk of its announced JDE Peet's acquisition ($8.5-8.7B in incremental net sales) combined with the subsequent plan to split into two separate pure-play companies (Beverage Co. and Global Coffee Co.). Near-term margin pressure in the coffee segment and the ballooning debt load from financing the deal have spooked income-oriented investors, even as core U.S. refreshment beverage brands are gaining share.
value_picks.bull_case: The corporate split is a well-telegraphed value unlock: the sum-of-parts of a pure-play U.S. beverage company and a global coffee compounder is significantly higher than today's blended multiple. KDP produced $1.5B in FCF in 2025 and guided to $2.0B in 2026 standalone, while management projects $400M in 3-year synergies from JDE Peet's. At 12.3x EV/EBITDA and a 4% FCF yield, investors are being compensated to wait through a complex but value-accretive transaction with a clear 30%+ upside path to analyst targets.

value_picks.name: Zoetis Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: ZTS fell ~32% from its 52-week high after management cut its 2026 outlook in February, citing a softening in U.S. veterinary clinic visits driven by cost-conscious Gen Z and Millennial pet owners. The Librela OA pain franchise — a key bull thesis — posted a steep 32% Q4 U.S. revenue decline, rattling investors who had priced in strong companion-animal drug growth. Additionally, the $160M Neogen animal genomics acquisition added to near-term leverage concerns amid an already elevated D/E ratio.
value_picks.bull_case: Zoetis remains the world's largest animal health company with irreplaceable diversification across 300+ products and 100+ countries, with international revenue growing 8% organically in 2025 to offset U.S. softness. Management guided 2026 FCF to jump 21.6% to $2.78B, and the $2.4B remaining buyback program signals strong capital return confidence. The renal care franchise targeting a $3-4B market enters approval in 2027, providing a durable next growth catalyst that the current ~15x EV/EBITDA multiple does not yet price.

value_picks.name: General Mills, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: General Mills issued a major guidance cut in February 2026, slashing its FY2026 organic sales outlook to -1.5% to -2% (from -1% to +1%) and reducing adjusted EPS guidance by an additional 5-10 points versus prior ranges, citing weak consumer sentiment and higher-than-expected volume recovery costs. Q3 FY2026 EPS of $0.64 missed by $0.09 and organic sales declined 3%, extending a 33% decline from the 52-week high to near multi-year lows.
value_picks.bull_case: At 13x P/E and a nearly 8% FCF yield — the highest in its peer group — GIS offers recession-proof earnings power from iconic brands (Cheerios, Yoplait, Nature Valley) at a deep value price. The company guided FCF conversion at 95%+ of adjusted after-tax earnings, and the Accelerate strategy's multi-year cost savings program should re-rate margins as volume recovers. In a deflationary macro regime with contracting growth, GIS is precisely the kind of non-discretionary staple that defensive portfolios rotate into, and the dividend yield above 4.5% provides a floor.

value_picks.name: Kimberly-Clark Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: KMB has fallen ~33% from its 52-week high after reporting retail sales declining 2.8% in February 2026 and management lowering FY2025 EPS guidance from $7.60 to $7.41 amid North American organic sales weakness. The stock suffered from a major North American Q3 2025 organic sales revision from +4.5% to -4.0% driven by pricing normalization post-hyperinflation and private-label competition in tissue and personal care categories, eroding investor confidence in near-term earnings power.
value_picks.bull_case: Kimberly-Clark's PowerUp strategy of portfolio simplification, SKU rationalization, and $1.5B productivity program targets a return to sustainable 2-4% organic growth and significant margin expansion by FY2027. The stock trades at 12.5x EV/EBITDA — a multi-decade low — with a nearly 5% FCF yield and a dividend yield above 4%. In a deflationary environment, KMB's non-discretionary tissue and personal care products face minimal demand destruction, and input cost tailwinds from lower oil prices and pulp should rebuild margins that were compressed during the inflationary cycle.

momentum_picks.name: Valero Energy Corporation
momentum_picks.rev_growth: -5.5% YoY (FY2025 revenue $122.7B; refining margin/barrel +61% YoY to $13.61 in Q4 2025)
momentum_picks.catalyst: Valero is the primary beneficiary of the Iran war-driven oil supply shock. With Brent crude surging above $115/bbl, refiners with complex heavy-sour feedstock capacity — like Valero's Gulf Coast operations — capture dramatically widened crack spreads. Q4 2025 adjusted EPS of $3.82 beat consensus by $0.55, and refining throughput hit a record 3.1 million bpd. UBS raised its price target to $280 on April 2 (from $215) and Wells Fargo lifted its target to $292, citing Valero's unique positioning to process Venezuelan heavy crude arriving in the U.S. as a Trump administration initiative progresses.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: On a forward FCF basis at current crack spreads, Valero trades at a mid-single-digit multiple — deeply discounted relative to the commodity environment. The primary risk is a rapid de-escalation of the Iran conflict or Saudi production increase that collapses crack spreads; historically these shocks reverse quickly and refiners give back gains faster than they accrued them. With the average analyst target at $239 (below current price of $244) and two bullish outliers at $280-$292, the near-term risk/reward skews slightly to the downside on mean-reversion of targets, but the geopolitical premium justifies a position given the 1-3 year horizon.

momentum_picks.name: Diamondback Energy, Inc.
momentum_picks.rev_growth: +35.8% YoY (FY2025 revenue $15.0B vs $11.1B in FY2024, driven by Endeavor acquisition consolidation)
momentum_picks.catalyst: Diamondback is the best-positioned Permian pure-play upstream beneficiary of $115/bbl oil, with 500-510 MBO/d of 2026 guided production generating approximately $4.3B in free cash flow at $60/bbl WTI — and materially more at current strip. The Barnett/Woodford position, a deeper unconventional zone beneath its Midland acreage, is delivering first-year oil recoveries of 75 barrels per lateral foot versus 50 for conventional Midland wells, a 50% productivity premium that represents zero optionality in Street models. The base dividend of $4.20/share is protected down to ~$37/bbl WTI, providing significant downside cushion. Every analyst covering FANG (8/8) carries a bullish rating with a median target of $222.50.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: FANG offers the cleanest levered-oil exposure on the buy side with disciplined capital allocation, a 4%+ free cash flow yield at spot, and an undervalued Barnett inventory that could re-rate reserves by 20%+ as development scales. The key risk is oil price: a drop back toward $60/bbl WTI (consensus base case for 2026 ex-war) would materially compress earnings and slow buybacks. With analysts in unanimous agreement and targets ranging from $190-$248 versus the April 2 close of $193.88, upside/downside is roughly 2:1 in a sustained oil environment.

momentum_picks.name: Northrop Grumman Corporation
momentum_picks.rev_growth: +2.2% YoY (FY2025 revenue $42.0B vs $41.0B in FY2024; guided mid-single-digit growth for 2026)
momentum_picks.catalyst: Northrop Grumman is the stealth bomber and missile defense incumbent that benefits most from a sustained high-intensity conflict with a sophisticated adversary like Iran. Shares surged 46% from the first U.S. strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities through early March 2026, driven by unprecedented demand for stealth aircraft, drone systems, and advanced radar — all Northrop's core competencies. CEO Kathy Warden has described an 'unprecedented demand cycle' across global defense markets. The company guided $3.1-3.5B in free cash flow for 2026 alongside mid-single-digit revenue growth, and the average analyst target of $765 (5/6 bullish) implies ~8.9% upside from current levels. The 1-month pullback (-7.5%) from the March 2 peak represents a constructive reset after a sharp gap-up on conflict escalation.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: NOC's business is driven by long-cycle government contracts, making it highly defensive in a prolonged conflict environment. The Iran war has removed political headwinds to expanded defense appropriations, and NATO allies are concurrently increasing budgets. The near-term risk is a peace settlement or ceasefire that deflates the defense premium; however, even in a peace scenario, the order backlog conversion cycle spans 3-5 years, providing durable revenue visibility. At 24x forward earnings for a high-single-digit FCF yield business with beta near zero, NOC screens as inexpensive relative to its quality and geopolitical tailwind.

momentum_picks.name: Agnico Eagle Mines Limited
momentum_picks.rev_growth: +43.7% YoY (FY2025 revenue $11.9B vs $8.3B in FY2024, driven by higher realized gold prices of $3,454/oz vs ~$2,300/oz in 2024)
momentum_picks.catalyst: Agnico Eagle is the highest-quality gold producer globally with the strongest balance sheet in company history: $2.9B net cash, zero net debt, $4.4B in record 2025 free cash flow, and a 12.5% dividend increase. With gold at $4,910/oz, AEM's all-in sustaining cost of ~$1,339/oz implies a margin of ~$3,571/oz — a 3x expansion versus 2024. Q4 2025 realized gold price of $4,163/oz already showed the operating leverage, and spot is $750/oz above that realized price. JP Morgan projects gold averaging $5,055/oz in Q4 2026. Record mineral reserves of 55.4 million oz (+2% YoY) and a growth pipeline targeting 4M+ oz/year by the early 2030s provide long-runway organic optionality. The 11% bounce in the past week signals market recognition of the pullback overshoot (-11% in March) amid gold mining sector rotation.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: AEM is the best-positioned vehicle to express a bullish gold view via fundamentally sound cash flow rather than pure commodity speculation. The deflation risk macro backdrop (a rare environment where gold can rally alongside falling bond yields) adds an additional tail to the gold thesis beyond the Iran-war safe-haven bid. The primary risk is a gold price reversal — AEM's earnings are highly sensitive to gold moves, and the stock tends to trade at a 2-3x multiple of gold's percentage move. Analyst targets range from $200-$276 with a mean of $230, providing 10% upside from April 2 close; the Scotiabank target of $276 implies 32% if gold continues its move toward $5,400/oz.

momentum_picks.name: Baker Hughes Company
momentum_picks.rev_growth: -0.3% YoY (FY2025 revenue $27.7B, essentially flat; IET segment backlog (RPO) hit a record $32.4B)
momentum_picks.catalyst: Baker Hughes is a structurally differentiated play: its Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) segment — which sells LNG equipment, gas compression, and data center power solutions — is growing rapidly and carries higher margins than its oilfield services business. The IET RPO hit a record $32.4B at year-end 2025, providing exceptional forward visibility. With Iran controlling the Strait of Hormuz and LNG demand from Europe and Asia surging for non-Middle Eastern supply, Baker Hughes is directly positioned to capture long-term LNG infrastructure orders. The $6.5B senior notes issuance in March 2026 funds capacity expansion into this LNG supercycle. The company's Q4 2025 adjusted profit rose 11% YoY, and analysts rate it Strong Buy at $58.50 average target with early-April sentiment improving.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: BKR offers a unique dual-catalyst profile: oil services upside from $115/bbl capex cycles plus structural IET growth driven by LNG and AI data centers (both power-hungry). At 23x forward earnings with a 1.5% dividend yield, the valuation is not cheap relative to its -0.3% top-line growth. However, the IET backlog conversion over 2026-2028 should drive a re-rating toward higher-multiple industrial comps. The main risk is IET margin disappointment or LNG project delays; OFSE (oilfield services) is a mixed picture as OPEC+ dynamics and non-OPEC growth could cap upstream capex growth. A sustained $115+ oil environment is bullish for both segments simultaneously.

trends.name: Geopolitical Risk as a Double-Edged Catalyst
trends.description: The Iran conflict has created a bifurcated market impact: energy and defense stocks are surging while consumer discretionary and transport names face oil cost headwinds. Equity markets rallied on reduced uncertainty after Trump's April 1 speech clarified a 2-3 week operational timeline, suggesting a defined rather than open-ended conflict. This dynamic — where geopolitical risk reduces tail-risk ambiguity — led to a 'buy the clarity' response from institutional investors.

trends.name: AI Infrastructure Cycle Driving Semiconductor and Storage Outperformance
trends.description: Storage and semiconductor names dominated the weekly winners, with SNDK (+13.9%), STX (+13.0%), MRVL (+12.9%), INTC (+16.8%), and CIEN (+11.5%) all posting double-digit weekly gains. The common thread is AI data center demand: NAND flash prices have risen ~25% from trough levels, HDD nearline demand is outpacing supply, and custom AI ASIC design wins are re-rating semiconductor companies like Marvell. This infrastructure buildout cycle appears durable, with hyperscalers continuing to expand CapEx guidance for FY2026.

trends.name: Cybersecurity Spending Accelerating on Geopolitical Urgency
trends.description: The Iran war escalation has created a tangible near-term catalyst for enterprise cybersecurity spending. PANW (+11.0%), RBRK (+12.5%), OKTA (+9.5%), and CRWD (+8.0%) all significantly outperformed the broader market. Palo Alto received an upgrade from KeyBanc on April 1, citing accelerating deal closures as enterprises moved from 'evaluation' to 'procurement' mode in response to elevated nation-state threat levels. This is a sector where geopolitical risk is a direct revenue tailwind.

trends.name: Consumer Discretionary Bifurcation: Execution vs. Macro
trends.description: Nike's -14% weekly decline illustrates the severe penalty for both missed execution and unfavorable macro exposure. With Greater China — historically Nike's highest-margin geography — guided down 20%, the market is reassessing the stock's medium-term earnings power. Meanwhile, health-oriented consumer brands and experiences continue to outperform. The consumer sector is increasingly bifurcating: premium/health brands with pricing power are holding up while mass-market footwear and apparel brands are struggling with demand elasticity and China exposure.

trends.name: Edge Compute and CDN Re-Rating as AI Inference Moves to the Edge
trends.description: Fastly's (+22% weekly, +58% 30-day) extraordinary move signals a broader market re-evaluation of edge compute infrastructure. As AI inference workloads scale and latency requirements tighten, the network edge — where CDN providers have existing PoPs and routing infrastructure — becomes a strategic deployment layer. DigitalOcean's NVIDIA GPU partnership (+55% 30-day) and Cloudflare's AI Gateway adoption (+17% 30-day) corroborate this theme. Investors are pricing AI inference distribution as a durable infrastructure category, not just a transient feature add.

risks.name: Sustained Oil Price Shock and Stagflationary Spillover
risks.description: WTI crude above $110/bbl and Brent above $109/bbl — driven by the Strait of Hormuz closure affecting ~20% of global oil flows — poses a meaningful stagflation risk if the conflict extends beyond the projected 2-3 week timeline. Energy input costs will feed through to transportation, manufacturing, and consumer prices with a 4-6 week lag. The Fed is effectively boxed in: rate cuts are difficult with oil-driven inflation, but rate hikes risk accelerating an already-slowing U.S. economy. A prolonged conflict could force a significant downward revision to 2026 EPS estimates across the S&P 500.

risks.name: China Demand Deterioration Across Multiple Sectors
risks.description: Nike's guided -20% Greater China Q4 revenue follows similar warnings from LVMH, Estée Lauder, and other consumer names in recent quarters. The pattern now appears structural rather than cyclical: domestic Chinese brands are taking share from foreign consumer labels at all price points. Technology companies with China exposure — including Qualcomm (~25% of revenue), NVIDIA (ADM chips), and Broadcom — face a related risk from export control escalation, particularly as the Iran conflict could prompt Beijing to exploit the distraction to press Taiwan Strait tensions.

risks.name: Mega-Cap Tech Valuation Reset Risk
risks.description: Despite this week's rally, the Nasdaq remains -5.86% YTD and Meta is -12% over 30 days. The mega-cap tech cohort (AMZN, GOOG, META, MSFT, NVDA) collectively trades at an average forward P/E of ~28x — elevated vs. the 10-year median of ~22x. If the Iran conflict drives a sustained risk-off shift or Q1 2026 earnings disappoint on ad revenue (META, GOOG) or cloud growth (MSFT, AMZN), the concentration of index weight in these five names creates a single-point-of-failure risk for index-level returns. Any material guidance cut from one of these names could trigger a broad de-risking event.

risks.name: Earnings Execution Risk in High-Multiple Software Names
risks.description: MongoDB fell 22% over 30 days, Sea Limited -22%, and Asana -13%, highlighting the unforgiving nature of high-multiple software valuations in the current environment. Q1 FY2026 earnings season for software kicks off in early May; consensus estimates embed ~18% average revenue growth for the cohort. Given macro uncertainty from oil prices and enterprise IT budget caution, any broad-based miss or guidance reduction could reprice the sector. The bar is particularly high for names trading above 10x forward revenue, where any deceleration triggers multiple compression.

risks.name: M&A Disruption Risk in Staples and Consumer
risks.description: The McCormick-Unilever Foods merger announcement (-30% 30-day for MKC, -22% for UL) illustrates how large strategic transactions can materially destroy short-term shareholder value even when strategic logic is sound. The current M&A environment — elevated financing costs, complex regulatory scrutiny, and anti-trust sensitivity — means that announced deals carry higher execution risk than in prior cycles. Investors should monitor other potential consolidation candidates in staples and consumer sectors for similar re-rating risk, particularly names where activist pressure has been building for transformative action.

watchlist_movers.summary: Nike reported Q3 FY2026 earnings after the close on March 31. Revenue was flat YoY at $11.3B; guidance for next quarter of -2% to -4% — well below consensus — drove a sharp selloff. Greater China guided down ~20%, adding to bearish sentiment.
watchlist_movers.detail: Nike's Q3 FY2026 results revealed ongoing structural challenges: Nike Direct revenue fell 4%, Converse dropped 35%, and gross margin contracted 130 bps to 40.2%. Management guided Q4 revenue at -2% to -4% and Greater China at -20%, citing macro headwinds and inventory normalization. The stock broke below the $50 level for the first time since 2018, wiping out roughly 14% in the week and 28% over 30 days. Analysts broadly cut price targets, with the consensus view shifting toward a prolonged recovery timeline of 12-18 months.

watchlist_movers.summary: McCormick reported Q1 FY2026 results on March 31 — revenue beat at $1.87B (+16.7%) with adj. EPS of $0.66 vs. $0.61 estimate. However, simultaneously announcing a merger with Unilever Foods to create a ~$20B combined entity sent the stock down ~8% over the week.
watchlist_movers.detail: McCormick's headline Q1 2026 numbers were solid: organic growth of 1.2% with 13% of revenue growth from the McCormick de Mexico acquisition. The company reaffirmed full-year guidance of +13-17% net sales growth and adj. EPS of $3.05-$3.13. The selloff was driven entirely by the Unilever Foods merger announcement, which investors viewed as a dilutive and complex integration risk. The 30-day decline of 30% largely reflects pre-announcement trading as investors de-risked around rumored deal talks.

watchlist_movers.summary: Fastly surged 22% on the week on no specific news, likely driven by short-covering and momentum after the stock had been consolidating at multi-year lows. The 30-day gain of 58% suggests a broader re-rating of edge compute/CDN assets in the AI-infrastructure buildout narrative.
watchlist_movers.detail: Fastly's 58% 30-day rally is one of the most striking moves in the watchlist. The company, which had been trading under $25 for much of early 2026, benefited from renewed investor interest in real-time edge computing as AI inference workloads increasingly move to the network edge. No specific earnings catalyst occurred this week; the move appears technically driven, with the stock breaking out above its 200-day moving average during the week. Short interest was elevated (~28% of float) heading into the week, amplifying the rally as bears covered positions.

watchlist_movers.summary: Intel surged 16.8% on the week after reports emerged that the company is exploring a sale of its foundry business, Intel Foundry Services, to TSMC or a private equity consortium. The report, first published Tuesday by Reuters, triggered a wave of buy-side activity.
watchlist_movers.detail: Intel's 30-day gain of 10.7% and sharp weekly move of 16.8% reflect a potential strategic restructuring that could unlock significant value. Separating or selling Intel Foundry Services — which has been the primary drag on margins and operating cash flow — would allow Intel to refocus on chip design, its historically higher-margin business. TSMC reportedly sees strategic value in U.S. manufacturing capacity given geopolitical pressure to build domestic fabs. The outcome remains uncertain; Intel management has neither confirmed nor denied the reports, but investors are pricing in meaningful probability of a deal.

watchlist_movers.summary: SOC surged 78.7% over 30 days before giving back 16.7% this week. The extreme move appears related to speculative trading around energy sector contract announcements rather than fundamental earnings.

Titan Machinery fell 15.7% on the week and 30 days after preannouncing Q4 FY2026 results well below consensus — revenue of $620M vs. $710M estimate — citing continued farm equipment demand weakness and elevated dealer inventory.

SanDisk gained 13.9% on the week, building on a 13% 30-day rally. The flash storage market has rebounded sharply as data center NAND demand — driven by AI training and inference storage — has pushed contract prices up for three consecutive months.
watchlist_movers.detail: SanDisk's 13% 30-day and 14% weekly moves reflect the ongoing recovery in NAND flash pricing, which bottomed in mid-2025 and has risen ~25% from trough levels. The company benefits directly as a pure-play NAND storage vendor; management guided for improving margins at the last earnings call as utilization rates at fabs approach 90%+. The data center AI buildout is creating durable demand for high-capacity NVMe SSDs, and hyperscalers have been pulling forward orders. Analyst consensus sees further upside: average price target sits ~15% above current levels, with bulls citing the multi-year AI storage cycle.

watchlist_movers.summary: Seagate Technology rose 13% on the week and 13% over 30 days, tracking the broader storage recovery thesis. HDD demand for AI data center nearline storage has been exceeding supply, and Seagate pre-announced upside for its fiscal Q3 2026 results.
watchlist_movers.detail: Seagate's pre-announcement of fiscal Q3 2026 revenue of $2.4B (vs. $2.2B guidance) and non-GAAP EPS of $1.95 (vs. $1.55 estimate) was the decisive catalyst. The upside was driven by AI hyperscaler nearline HDD demand — Seagate's 32TB+ HAMR drives are seeing accelerating orders as AI training datasets scale beyond SSD cost-efficiency thresholds. At $429/share, Seagate is pricing in a multi-year HDD upcycle. CEO Dave Mosley noted supply tightness may persist through 2027 given limited incremental capacity investments from the industry during the prior downcycle.

watchlist_movers.summary: Marvell Technology gained 12.9% on the week and 32% over 30 days, driven by sustained optimism around its custom AI ASIC business serving hyperscalers. Morgan Stanley reiterated Overweight with a $130 target early in the week.
watchlist_movers.detail: Marvell's 30-day rally of 32% reflects growing investor conviction that its custom silicon (ASIC) design wins at Amazon AWS, Microsoft Azure, and potentially Google are durable multi-year revenue streams. The company guided for AI-related revenue to double in FY2027 at its February analyst day. At $107/share, Marvell trades at ~28x forward earnings — a premium that the market appears willing to pay given the secular AI infrastructure tailwind. Semiconductor peers like Broadcom have validated the custom ASIC monetization model, which investors are now pricing into Marvell's growth trajectory.

watchlist_movers.summary: Rubrik surged 12.5% on the week — one of the strongest performers in cybersecurity. Short-covering likely contributed as the stock had been heavily shorted heading into this week; the geopolitical security spending backdrop is directionally positive for Rubrik's data security platform.

Ciena surged 11.5% on the week and 26.6% over 30 days after reporting strong fiscal Q1 2026 results on March 6. Revenue of $1.06B beat consensus by 8%, and the company raised FY2026 guidance citing accelerating fiber and data center interconnect orders.
watchlist_movers.detail: Ciena's Q1 FY2026 results confirmed that the optical networking upcycle is real and accelerating. Management pointed to hyperscaler data center interconnect as the fastest-growing segment, now representing over 30% of revenue. Gross margins expanded 180 bps YoY to 46.3% as the product mix shifts toward higher-margin software-defined WaveLogic optics. The 26% 30-day move reflects investors upgrading their FY2026 EPS estimates materially upward following the guidance raise. Key risk: order concentration — the top 3 customers represent ~65% of revenue, creating lumpiness.

watchlist_movers.summary: Palo Alto Networks gained 11% on the week, outperforming the cybersecurity sector. The Iran war escalation drove elevated enterprise security urgency, boosting Palo Alto's pipeline visibility. KeyBanc upgraded to Overweight on April 1.

GitLab gained 10.9% on the week, recovering from a 13.8% 30-day decline. The bounce follows oversold technical conditions after the stock was hit by growth concerns in February earnings. AI-assisted DevSecOps adoption remains a key growth driver.

AppLovin declined 10.8% on the week and 30 days, giving back gains from its January highs. The primary concern is increasing competition in mobile performance advertising from Google's newly launched Performance Max Mobile and emerging TikTok ads API integrations.

DigitalOcean climbed 10.6% on the week and 55% over 30 days. The company announced a partnership with NVIDIA on March 10 to offer GPU cloud instances for AI startups, which has been the primary driver of the 30-day move.
watchlist_movers.detail: DigitalOcean's 55% 30-day rally is the largest sustained gain among cloud infrastructure names in the watchlist. The NVIDIA partnership announced March 10 made DigitalOcean a go-to platform for AI startups seeking affordable GPU access without enterprise-tier pricing or lengthy contract negotiations. Analysts at Needham upgraded to Buy (from Hold) with a $110 target, citing an estimated $200M TAM expansion from the GPU product line. The stock's continued rise through April 2 suggests institutional accumulation rather than short-covering. Key risk is execution — DigitalOcean must demonstrate GPU revenue contribution in Q1 FY2026 earnings (due mid-May).

watchlist_movers.summary: Okta rose 9.5% on the week as investors rotated into identity security plays amid the Iran war escalation. The company has been executing well post-Oktane conference, with its Okta AI and Identity Governance products showing strong pipeline traction.

Meta Platforms rallied 9.3% on the week, recovering from a 12% 30-day drawdown. The bounce appears technically driven, with buyers returning after Meta tested its 200-day moving average. AI ad targeting remains the core investment thesis.

Samsara rose 8.4% on the week and 10.8% over 30 days. The company has been benefiting from strong fleet telematics demand and an expansion of its AI dashcam product following a new FMCSA safety mandate effective March 1, 2026.
watchlist_movers.detail: Samsara's gains reflect a fundamental demand catalyst: the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) mandate requiring AI-enabled forward-facing dashcams for commercial fleets with 50+ vehicles, effective March 1, 2026, has created a compliance-driven upgrade cycle for Samsara's core product. The company estimated a $2B+ incremental TAM from the mandate at its November investor day. ARR growth accelerated to 37% in Q4 FY2025, and management indicated that mandate-related orders were already showing up in Q1 FY2026 pipeline data. The 30-day move is supported by buy-side consensus upgrading estimates for the next two to three quarters.

watchlist_movers.summary: CrowdStrike gained 8% on the week, benefiting from the same geopolitical security tailwind as peers. The stock has broadly recovered from its mid-February earnings-driven pullback, with management's platform consolidation thesis gaining traction.
AMD rose 7.7% on the week on continued momentum in its MI300X GPU business and positive sell-side commentary about its AI server market share gains vs. NVIDIA.

Alphabet rallied 7.6% on the week, snapping a multi-week downtrend. Cloud and AI product momentum, particularly Gemini model integrations in Google Workspace, helped reassure investors after a soft early quarter.
Western Digital gained 7.1% on the week, tracking Seagate's pre-announcement and the broader storage recovery. WDC benefits from both NAND flash pricing recovery and HDD enterprise demand.

UnitedHealth Group recovered 7% on the week after several weeks of underperformance. No company-specific news; the move appears sector rotation driven as healthcare came back into favor with risk-on sentiment.

Unilever fell 6.6% on the week and 22.4% over 30 days after announcing its merger with McCormick's food division. Investors viewed the deal as expensive and strategically complex, particularly given Unilever's ongoing transformation under its new CEO.
Tenable rose 6.3% on the week amid elevated enterprise cybersecurity demand driven by geopolitical tensions. The stock remains down 10% over 30 days after its February earnings miss.

CF Industries gained 24.6% over 30 days, driven by the Iran war escalation lifting natural gas and ammonia prices globally. As a nitrogen fertilizer producer, CF benefits directly from elevated input cost spreads when finished fertilizer prices rise faster than natural gas feedstock costs.
watchlist_movers.detail: CF Industries' 24.6% 30-day gain reflects the market pricing in a structural commodity tailwind from the Middle East conflict. Iran is a significant natural gas producer; the military conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption have tightened global LNG supply. CF benefits from its U.S. natural gas cost advantage: while European and Asian ammonia producers face surging input costs, CF's Henry Hub-linked feedstock remains relatively cheaper, expanding its margin advantage. The weekly pullback of 4.75% reflects some profit-taking as traders assessed whether the oil/gas spike would be sustained or short-lived.

watchlist_movers.summary: Cloudflare advanced 4.3% on the week and 16.9% over 30 days. The 30-day move has been driven by rising demand for its AI Gateway and Workers AI products, with several analyst upgrades since early March citing Cloudflare as a key beneficiary of enterprise AI security and inference routing.
watchlist_movers.detail: Cloudflare's 30-day rally of 17% reflects growing conviction that its Zero Trust + AI platform is winning mid-market enterprise deals. Barclays upgraded to Overweight ($250 target) in mid-March citing pipeline data from channel checks showing accelerating AI Gateway adoption. The stock has also benefited from the broader cybersecurity sector re-rating as the Iran war escalation increased enterprise urgency around network security investments. Cloudflare's developer-first distribution model keeps customer acquisition costs low, supporting durable net revenue retention rates above 115%. Next catalyst: Q1 FY2026 earnings in early May.

watchlist_movers.summary: Arm Holdings gained 3.5% on the week and 19.9% over 30 days. The 30-day move reflects sustained AI-driven royalty growth expectations and SoftBank's disclosure that it plans to hold its Arm stake through 2028.
watchlist_movers.detail: Arm's 20% 30-day rally continues the stock's recovery from its February correction. The primary driver is investor confidence in Arm's royalty ramp: as the industry migrates to Arm v9 architecture (which carries 2x the royalty rate of v8), revenue mix improvement should be visible in coming quarters. Additionally, Arm's expanding presence in data center CPUs — with AWS Graviton, Ampere Computing, and NVIDIA's Grace processor all shipping in volume — creates a secular AI tailwind independent of mobile cycles. The stock remains richly valued at ~60x forward earnings; the bull case requires sustained royalty rate expansion and AI data center penetration.

watchlist_movers.summary: Braze rose 2.96% on the week but 27% over 30 days, largely driven by its February earnings beat (Q4 FY2025) where it reported 22% revenue growth and raised FY2026 guidance above consensus.
watchlist_movers.detail: Braze's 27% 30-day move is a continuation of its post-earnings rerating from the February 6 report. The company reported $152M in Q4 FY2025 revenue (up 22% YoY), non-GAAP operating income of $11M (its third consecutive profitable quarter), and guided Q1 FY2026 revenue at $154-155M vs. $152M consensus. The combination of durable top-line growth, expanding operating leverage, and a strong NRR of 116% drove a re-rating from growth-at-a-discount to a premium SaaS multiple. The stock has continued grinding higher in the weeks since, as investors rotate into profitable growth names within software.

watchlist_movers.summary: Hims & Hers Health was up 16.1% over 30 days despite a modest -1.2% dip this week. The 30-day move follows a series of positive announcements around GLP-1 compounding supply agreements and expansion of its telehealth platform.
watchlist_movers.detail: HIMS has been a volatile mover throughout Q1 2026. The 16% 30-day gain reflects investor optimism around the company's GLP-1 (semaglutide and tirzepatide) compounded medication business, which had faced regulatory uncertainty earlier in 2025. The FDA's confirmation that compound pharmacies can continue supplying GLP-1 medications while branded shortage designations remain in place was a significant positive. HIMS also expanded its mental health and dermatology offerings, diversifying beyond the original men's health franchise. Key risk: Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly are aggressively ramping Ozempic/Wegovy production, which could end shortage designations and eliminate HIMS's compounding opportunity within 12-18 months.
